methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Summit Nanotech, a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Amanda Hall, is valued using a peer-median approach grounded in a Climate & Energy sector comp set of 3 same-stage private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA). The target has raised approximately C$120M to date.

The peer-median method applies directly given Summit's positioning within a defined cohort of Series A climate tech investors. Three matched comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—establish a median valuation of $150M, with a documented range of $110M to $200M. This single triangulation method, supported by available comparable data, yields an implied value of $150M. The broader comp universe includes 8 private peers (including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials) and 1 public reference point, providing contextual market validation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered at $150M. This range reflects the peer median anchored by the comparable set's interquartile spread. Key uncertainties include the limited methodological diversity—only peer-median analysis was applicable given available data—and the narrow peer sample of three companies. Sector-specific execution risk and differentiation relative to the comparable set remain unquantified in this analysis. The derived range should be stress-tested against Summit's technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market timing relative to identified peers.
